runs.notes: First run under the new reporting spec. Bootstrapped data/ storage layer (did not previously exist in repo). Backfilled site totals, device split, and channel split for 2026-07-06 through 2026-07-14 from the GA4 Funnel Digital & Adnoc exploration via manual date-range queries (no GA4 API/connector available in this environment). Per-day Overall Funnel and Adnoc Funnel step data was collected directly for 2026-07-13 and 2026-07-14, and as an aggregate (not per-day) for This Week So Far (2026-07-13 to 2026-07-14) and Last Week (2026-07-06 to 2026-07-12). Per-day funnel step breakdowns for 2026-07-06 through 2026-07-12 individually were NOT collected (would require 7 additional GA4 exploration queries) and are stored as null in those daily files rather than estimated or interpolated, per the no-estimation rule.
missing_data: new_users (site) - metric not collected this run for any date
engaged_sessions / engagement_rate (site) - metric not collected this run for any date
per-day Overall Funnel and Adnoc Funnel step counts for 2026-07-06 through 2026-07-12 (only the 7-day aggregate for Last Week is available)

runs.notes: Recurring daily update. Checked GitHub first per spec: data/daily/2026-07-15.json was missing (latest stored day was 2026-07-14), so collected only 2026-07-15 data from the GA4 Funnel Digital & Adnoc exploration (site/device/channel totals via the Device Category tab and Channel Acquisition tab, Overall Funnel via the Overall Funnel exploration tab, Adnoc Funnel via the Adnoc Funnel exploration tab, all filtered to the single date 15 Jul 2026). No previously validated daily JSON files were reopened or modified. This Week So Far now has full per-day Overall/Adnoc funnel data for all three days (13, 14, 15 Jul), so This Week totals in the report are computed as the sum of the three daily files rather than a separate range query, which is a more accurate figure than the prior run's 2-day range aggregate. Last Week (6-12 Jul) remains the locked 7-day aggregate from the initial setup run and was not recollected.
missing_data: new_users (site) - metric not collected this run for any date
engaged_sessions / engagement_rate (site) - metric not collected this run for any date
per-day Overall Funnel and Adnoc Funnel step counts for 2026-07-06 through 2026-07-12 (only the 7-day aggregate for Last Week is available, collected in the initial setup run)

runs.notes: Recurring daily update. Checked GitHub first per spec: data/daily/2026-07-16.json was missing (latest stored day was 2026-07-15, validated), so collected only 2026-07-16 data from the GA4 Funnel Digital & Adnoc exploration (site/device/channel totals via the Device Category Analysis tab and Channel Acquisition tab, Overall Funnel via the Overall Funnel exploration tab with segment comparison and device breakdown removed to isolate All Users totals, Adnoc Funnel via the Adnoc Funnel exploration tab, all filtered to the single date 16 Jul 2026). No previously validated daily JSON files were reopened or modified. This Week So Far now has full per-day Overall/Adnoc funnel data for all four days (13, 14, 15, 16 Jul), so This Week totals in the report are computed as the sum of the four daily files. Last Week (6-12 Jul) remains the locked 7-day aggregate from the initial setup run and was not recollected. Validated: purchases (147) <= Payment Page users (202); funnel order strictly decreasing; no placeholders; report title/date matches data date.

runs.notes: Recurring daily update after a multi-run gap (last run was 2026-07-17 for data_date 2026-07-16; no runs occurred for 2026-07-17 or 2026-07-18). Checked GitHub first per spec: latest stored/validated day was 2026-07-16. Per the one-new-day-per-run efficiency rule, collected only 2026-07-19 (yesterday) from the GA4 Funnel Digital & Adnoc exploration (Device Category Analysis tab for site/device totals, Channel Acquisition tab with First user primary channel group dimension for channel split, Overall Funnel tab and Adnoc Funnel tab each filtered to the single date 19 Jul 2026). Deliberately did NOT collect 2026-07-17 or 2026-07-18 this run. site.purchases uses the true GA4 Purchases metric (event count of lowercase purchase event, verified against the Event name breakdown: 481 all-users / 12 Adnoc for 19 Jul, matching the standard Purchases metric exactly), consistent with the 2026-07-17 correction methodology recorded in data/config.json; overall_funnel.purchase and adnoc_funnel.purchase remain funnel-step completion counts (92 / 10) and the live report continues to display Purchases/Purchase Conversion Rate using the funnel-step count, consistent with how 07-06 through 07-16 are displayed. Because 17-18 Jul are missing, the Yesterday vs Previous Day section of the report compares 19 Jul against the last available validated day (16 Jul, 3 days prior) instead of a true consecutive previous day, and this is clearly labeled in the report header, tables and data quality section rather than presented as a normal day-over-day figure. This Week figures are the average of the 5 available days (13,14,15,16,19 Jul) rather than a full 7-day total; Last Week (6-12 Jul) remains the locked 7-day aggregate and was not recollected. Validated before publish: purchases (92) <= Payment Page users (141) for Overall funnel; Adnoc purchases (10) <= Adnoc Payment Page (15); funnel order strictly decreasing (Overall) / non-increasing (Adnoc); no placeholders or estimated values; report title/date matches data date (19 Jul 2026); charts match tables; no horizontal scroll issues introduced.
missing_data: 2026-07-17 daily data - not collected this run (one-new-day-per-run policy after a multi-day automation gap)

2026-07-18 daily data - not collected this run (same reason); Previous Day and This Week comparisons are therefore based on 16 Jul and a 5-day average respectively, not true adjacent-day/full-week figures
new_users (site) - metric not collected this run for any date
engaged_sessions / engagement_rate (site) - metric not collected this run for any date
per-day Overall Funnel and Adnoc Funnel step counts for 2026-07-06 through 2026-07-12 (only the 7-day aggregate for Last Week is available, collected in the initial setup run)

runs.notes: Recurring daily update. Checked GitHub first per spec: latest stored/validated day was 2026-07-19, so per the one-new-day-per-run efficiency rule, collected only 2026-07-20 (yesterday) from the GA4 Funnel Digital & Adnoc exploration (Device Category Analysis tab for site/device totals and revenue, Channel Acquisition tab with First user primary channel group dimension for channel split, Overall Funnel tab and Adnoc Funnel tab each filtered to the single date 20 Jul 2026). Because 2026-07-19 is already validated and stored, Previous Day for this run is a true consecutive day (19 Jul), unlike the prior run which had to compare against a 3-day-stale day. site.purchases uses the true GA4 Purchases metric (event count of lowercase 'purchase' event: 780 all-users / 15 Adnoc for 20 Jul), consistent with the 2026-07-17 correction methodology recorded in data/config.json; overall_funnel.purchase and adnoc_funnel.purchase remain funnel-step completion counts (148 / 13) and the live report continues to display Purchases/Purchase Conversion Rate using the funnel-step count, consistent with all prior dates. 2026-07-17 and 2026-07-18 remain uncollected (this policy does not backfill old gaps); the Last Week (13-19 Jul) comparison window therefore remains a 5-of-7-day average, unchanged from the prior run. This Week is now the new week starting 2026-07-20 (Monday) with only 1 of 7 days collected so far. Validated before publish: purchases (148) <= Payment Page users (210) for Overall funnel; Adnoc purchases (13) <= Adnoc Payment Page (19); funnel order strictly decreasing in both funnels; no placeholders or estimated values; report title/date matches data date (20 Jul 2026); charts match tables; no horizontal scroll issues introduced.
missing_data: 2026-07-17 daily data - never collected (pre-existing gap from a prior automation lapse, not addressed this run per no-backfill-of-old-gaps policy)
2026-07-18 daily data - same reason; Last Week (13-19 Jul) comparisons are therefore based on a 5-day average, not a full 7-day week
